title: OutcomeManifest
description: The oracle ground truth for a fixture: what a correct decomposition MUST contain to achieve this plan's outcomes. This is what makes 'were all the edges caught?' and 'is every needed bead present?' falsifiable. Hand-authored alongside the fixture, hash-pinned, never seen by a strategy.

fixture.description: fixture name, must match the directory

outcomes.description: the end-states the plan promises; each must be reachable to >=1 buildable bead

satisfiedByAnyOf.description: planKeys/requirement-ids any of which, if covered by a bead, satisfies this outcome

requirements.description: must-exist work units derived from outcomes; each must be covered by >=1 bead

planKey.description: the provenance key a covering bead must carry

feature.description: OPTIONAL: the feature this requirement belongs to. On a multi-feature fixture this is what lets requiredEdges be partitioned into intra-feature (library-covered) vs inter-feature (seam).

surfaces.description: the surfaces this plan must touch (e.g. db-schema, api-route, ui-view, worker, deploy, tests, docs)

concerns.description: cross-cutting concerns (e.g. auth, validation, error-handling) that must be addressed (or explicitly excluded)

mustHaves.description: explicit must-have features; each must map to >=1 delivering bead

requiredEdges.description: the COMPLETE expected ordering-dependency set, expressed between planKeys (not bead ids). Every one of these must be realized as a real (transitive) edge in the snapshot. Missing one = a caught/uncaught-edge defect.

partition.description: OPTIONAL: which class of dependency this edge is. intra-feature = both endpoints inside one feature (library-coverable, the EASY edges). seam = endpoints in DIFFERENT features (the hard, commonly-missed, anchoring-sensitive edges a per-feature template cannot hold). ordering-only / policy-only = not resource-mediated (won't fall out of the join — see Step 1). The runner reports library-covered-vs-seam edge recall separately when this tag is present.
